## [00:00 - 01:04]

The way marketing was set up when I kind of started my journey was perfect. You didn't have to create videos. You didn't have to take pictures of yourself, selfies, all this kind of stuff. If you want to blog and just write and hide behind that, you could. And I got a ton of traffic and thrived off of it. And then things started to evolve a little bit and I resisted for a long time. I dab a little bit in long form video, but I just couldn't do short form video. Whenever I would try to, I would do something where I wouldn't end up on screen. I would just like do a screen share or something like that. But there kind of became this like this make or break moment last summer, where I was like, I got to do something different. This isn't working completely committed to video, short form video. It's almost a year ago now, September 30th. Like everybody else who hasn't got into it. I was so intimidated by it because I'm like, what am I going to talk about? What I got to do about my lighting, the camera, I don't know how to edit. And like you jump into like TikTok or something like all this, all this, these editing things in there and like, what is this?
